name: Apple Streusel Squares
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly grease sides of a 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Prepare Streusel Topping: In a medium bowl, mix flour, pecans, brown sugar, slightly softened butter, vanilla, and cinnamon until the mixture comes together. Shape into a ball.
Prepare crust: Into a large bowl, measure flour, sugar, and salt. Cut in 1/2 cup cold butter into small bits. With fingertips, blend butter into flour mixture until it resembles fine crumbs. Press evenly into the bottom of the baking pan. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden. The crust may crack slightly; do not worry.
Prepare apples: Place apple slices in a skillet. Add raisins, brown sugar, cinnamon, and 3 tablespoons butter. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until apples are tender. In a small bowl, mix cornstarch and lemon juice. Stir the lemon juice mixture into the apple slice mixture and cook until thickened (this should happen quickly). Remove from heat.
Assemble: Spoon the apple mixture over the crust. Break the streusel topping into chunks and scatter over the apple mixture. Bake for 30 minutes or until the streusel is browned. Allow to cool completely before cutting into 16 pieces and serving. Serves 16.
